summary:
When the Rb gene cannot be normally phosphorylated, it fails to inhibit the transition from G1 to S phase, allowing uncontrolled cell proliferation. This leads to excessive cellular division, aligning with option C where the cell cycle progresses uncontrollably resulting in division. The absence or malfunction of proper Rb regulation permits continuous activation of pathways necessary for entering new rounds of the cell cycle independently of normal checkpoints. To summarize concisely while maintaining accuracy:

When Rb can't get correctly phosphorylated, it loses ability to stop moving past G1-S checkpoint; thus leading to rampant cell multiplication fitting description given in choice C perfectly—cell cycle advances unabated causing repeated divisions. Key point being loss function at heart disrupts natural controls over timing & frequency dividing events essential keeping tissues healthy preventing cancerous growth patterns developing instead.
